MOVING PARTY IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.6B AND PROVIDE COURTESY COPY OF THE NOTICE OF MOTION TO HOLD RESPONDENTS IN CONTEMPT. OPPOSING PARTY IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.6B AND PROVIDE COURTESY COPY OF THE RESPONSE. COURTESY COPIES MUST BE DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 501 NO LATER THAN JANUARY 22, 2014 BY 4 P.M. WITH COVER LETTER REFLECTING NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT IS IN RECEIPT OF THE MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION, DECLARATION OF STEPHEN PORTER AND REPLY. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK J. RIOS MERIDA, NOT REPORTED. (501)

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, AFTER HEARING ARGUMENTS FROM PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY JUDGMENT DEBTORS PAUL MARTIN AND SHARON YOST, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY, SHOULD NOT BE HELD IN CONTEMPT IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 18, 2014. PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER TO CHOSE 3 CONTRACTORS, ONE OF WHICH SHOULD BE SPRYSCAPES (IF WILLING AND AVAILABLE) TO PROVIDE ESTIMATES REGARDING WALL CORRECTION IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE ARBITRATION AWARD/JUDGMENT AND TO DETERMINE WHETHER CORRECTION IS POSSIBLE WITHOUT DAMAGE TO OR REBUILDING OF THE PLAINITFF'S WALL. IF PARTIES CANNOT AGREE, THEN EACH PARTY SHALL DESIGNATE ONE CONTRACTOR IN ADDITION TO SPRYSCAPES (IF SPRYSCAPES IS NOT AVAILABLE DEFENDANTS SHALL DESIGNATE TWO). BOTH PLAINTIFFS AND DEFENDANTS MUST PROVIDE ACCESS TO THE CHOSEN CONTRACTORS ON A 24 HOUR WRITTEN NOTICE DURING THE WEEK OF MARCH 24, 2014 FOR THE PURPOSES OUTLINED ABOVE. CONTRACTORS MUST BE CHOSEN NO LATER THAN MARCH 17, 2014. PARTIES SHALL FILE JOINT OR SEPARATE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING NOT TO EXCEED 3 PAGES NO LATER THAN MARCH 20, 2014 PROVIDING THE COURT WITH THE LIST OF CONTRACTORS AND TENTATIVE DATES FOR ESTIMATES. PARTIES SHALL FILE FURTHER SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING NO TO EXCEED 5 PAGES EXCLUDING EXHIBITS NO LATER THAN APRIL 11, 2014 PROVIDING THE COURT WITH INFORMATION REGARDING ESTIMATES AND AVAILABILITIES OF THE CHOSEN CONTRACTORS TO PERFORM THE WORK DURING THE MONTHS OF MAY THROUGH JULY 2014. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. THE COURT WILL RESERVE RULING ON THE COMPTEMPT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE LILIAN K. SING, CLERK B. PAREDES, NOT REPORTED. (501)

Order To Show Cause Why Judgment Debtors Paul Martin And Sharon Yost, Jointly And Severally, Should Not Be Held In Contempt
Off Calendar Apr-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stephen L. Porter, Esq of Whitehead & Porter, LLP (415-781-6070) for Vinod Kripalani, Petitioner.
Paul Martin and Sharon Yost, (415-567-8000), Respondents in Pro Per.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: Order To Show Cause Why Judgment Debtors Paul Martin And Sharon Yost, Jointly And Severally, Should Not Be Held In Contempt is GRANTED in full. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge: Lillian K. Sing, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

Order To Show Cause Why Judgment Debtors Paul Martin And Sharon Yost, Jointly And Severally, Should Not Be Held In Contempt
Off Calendar Mar-06-2014 Continued To Apr-18-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stephen Lyle Porter, Esq. of Whitehead & Porter LLP (415-781-6070) for Vinod Kripalani, Plaintiff & moving party.
No appearance by defendants & opposing party.

After hearing arguments from plaintiff's counsel, The Court Adopts the Tentative ruling as follows: Order To Show Cause Why Judgment Debtors Paul Martin And Sharon Yost, Jointly And Severally, Should Not Be Held In Contempt is Continued to April 18, 2014. Parties are to meet and confer to chose 3 contractors, one of which should be Spryscapes (if willing and available) to provide estimates regarding wall correction in compliance with the arbitration award/judgment and to determine whether correction is possible without damage to or rebuilding of the Plainitff's wall. If parties cannot agree, then each party shall designate one contractor in addition to Spryscapes (if Spryscapes is not available defendants shall designate two). Both Plaintiffs and Defendants must provide access to the chosen contractors on a 24 hour written notice during the week of March 24, 2014 for the purposes outlined above. Contractors must be chosen no later than March 17, 2014. Parties shall file joint or separate supplemental briefing not to exceed 3 pages no later than March 20, 2014 providing the Court with the list of contractors and tentative dates for estimates. Parties shall file further supplemental briefing no to exceed 5 pages excluding exhibits no later than April 11, 2014 providing the Court with information regarding estimates and availabilities of the chosen contractors to perform the work during the months of May through July 2014. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. The Court will reserve ruling on the Comptempt. Prevailing party to prepare order approved as to form. Judge Lilian K. Sing, Clerk B. Paredes, Not Reported. (501)

Judge: James A. Robertson II, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. (D501)
